title: Vadakkunnathan Temple, Thrissur
tagline: Vadakkunnathan Temple, Thrissur — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: The Vadakkumnathan Temple is an ancient Hindu temple dedicated to Shiva in Thrissur, in the Thrissur district of Kerala, India.
overview.description: The Vadakkumnathan Temple is an ancient Hindu temple dedicated to Shiva in Thrissur, in the Thrissur district of Kerala, India. The temple is a classical example of the architectural style of Kerala and has one monumental tower on each of the four sides in addition to a koothambalam. Mural paintings depicting various scenes from the Mahabharata can be seen inside the temple. The shrines and the Kuttambalam display vignettes carved in wood.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Thekkinkadu Maidan
topPlaces.description: Thekkinkadu Maidanam is a historic open ground located in the center of Thrissur city in the Kerala state of India. The 65-acre area surrounds the ancient Vadakkunnathan Temple, one of the most significant Shiva temples in Kerala, and is a vital cultural and historical landmark.

topPlaces.name: Jos Theatre
topPlaces.description: Jos Theatre is the first permanent movie theatre in Kerala, India. Located in Swaraj Round in Thrissur city, it was built by Kattukkaran Varunny Joseph, the first man to screen the film in Kerala.

topPlaces.name: Swaraj Round, Thrissur
topPlaces.description: Swaraj Round or Thrissur Round is the local name for a circular road which is about 2 km long in the centre of the city of Thrissur in Kerala, India, surrounding the 65-acre (260,000 m2) hillock called the Thekkinkadu Maidan which seats the Vadakkumnathan Temple. Swaraj Round is the biggest Roundabout in South Asia.

topPlaces.name: Thrissur
topPlaces.description: Thrissur (Malayalam: തൃശൂർ, pronounced [triˈʃ(ː)uːr] ), formerly Trichur, also known by its historical name Thrissivaperur, is a city and the headquarters of the Thrissur district in Kerala, India. It is the third largest urban agglomeration in Kerala after Kochi and Kozhikode, and the 21st largest in India.

topPlaces.name: Paramekkavu Bagavathi Temple
topPlaces.description: Paramekkavu Bagavathi Temple is one of the largest Bagavathi temples in Kerala located in Thrissur City. Sakthan Thampuran ordained the temples into two groups, namely "Paramekkavu side" and "Thiruvambady side" for Thrissur Pooram which is the biggest festival in South India and Kerala.

topPlaces.name: Basilica of Our Lady of Dolours, Thrissur
topPlaces.description: Our Lady of Dolours Basilica alias Puthenpally (Malayalam: പുത്തൻപള്ളി, meaning: New Church) is a minor basilica of the Syro-Malabar Catholic Church in Thrissur City in Kerala, India. It is the tallest church in India and has the tallest continuous church tower in Asia.

image.alt: Basilica of Our Lady of Dolours, Thrissur

topPlaces.name: Vadakkechira
topPlaces.description: Vadakkechira (Malayalam: വടക്കെചിറ) is one of the four oldest ponds in Thrissur city of Kerala in India. It was built by Shakthan Thampuran (1751-1805) and is one of Thrissur's famous landmarks.

topPlaces.name: Vadakke Madham Brahmaswam Vedic Research Centre
topPlaces.description: Vadakke Madham Brahmaswam Vedic Research Centre is a residential institution for the study of vedas. It is located on the compound of Vadakke Madhom in Thrissur city of Kerala state in India.

image.alt: Vadakke Madham Brahmaswam Vedic Research Centre

items.note: Thekkinkadu Maidanam is a historic open ground located in the center of Thrissur city in the Kerala state of India. The 65-acre area surrounds the ancient Vadakkunnathan Temple, one of the most significant Shiva temples in Kerala, and is a vital cultural and historical landmark.
Jos Theatre is the first permanent movie theatre in Kerala, India. Located in Swaraj Round in Thrissur city, it was built by Kattukkaran Varunny Joseph, the first man to screen the film in Kerala.

Swaraj Round is the biggest Roundabout in South Asia.

Thrissur (Malayalam: തൃശൂർ, pronounced [triˈʃ(ː)uːr] ), formerly Trichur, also known by its historical name Thrissivaperur, is a city and the headquarters of the Thrissur district in Kerala, India. It is the third largest urban agglomeration in Kerala after Kochi and Kozhikode, and the 21st largest in India.

Paramekkavu Bagavathi Temple is one of the largest Bagavathi temples in Kerala located in Thrissur City. Sakthan Thampuran ordained the temples into two groups, namely "Paramekkavu side" and "Thiruvambady side" for Thrissur Pooram which is the biggest festival in South India and Kerala.

Vadakkechira (Malayalam: വടക്കെചിറ) is one of the four oldest ponds in Thrissur city of Kerala in India. It was built by Shakthan Thampuran (1751-1805) and is one of Thrissur's famous landmarks.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Vadakkunnathan Temple, Thrissur?
faq.a: The best time to visit Vadakkunnathan Temple, Thrissur is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Vadakkunnathan Temple, Thrissur?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Cochin International Airport, Kochi (46 km) and the nearest railway station is Ernakulam Junction (Kochi) (62 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Vadakkunnathan Temple, Thrissur?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Vadakkunnathan Temple, Thrissur at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Vadakkunnathan Temple, Thrissur?
faq.a: Stays in Vadakkunnathan Temple, Thrissur start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Vadakkunnathan Temple, Thrissur?
faq.a: The top attractions are Thekkinkadu Maidan, Jos Theatre, Swaraj Round, Thrissur, Thrissur, Paramekkavu Bagavathi Temple.
